Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com/ 2. Navigate to Reports > Expenses 3. Copy this: type:expense-report action:submit from:me (note: any query using from:me does not work) 4. Paste it on the search field 'Search for something' and press Enter, or select the first highlighted option. ## Expected Result: After pasting type:expense-report action:submit from:me and selecting the first highlighted option or pressing Enter, me should turn green, and the system should correctly apply the filter, returning only the expense reports submitted by the current user. ## Actual Result: When the query type:expense-report action:submit from:me is used, me does not turn green, meaning it is not detected. The system redirects to the expected page but fails to filter by me, showing unfiltered results instead ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ca07470-efea-49da-978b-f94683b9a54a [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] SmartScan Data Not Applied to Expenses When Instant Submit Is Enabled (Race Condition)

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @Kel-Expensify **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1774566638215229) ## Action Performed: 1. Enable Instant Submit on a policy 2. Create a new expense with a receipt 3. Observe that: The report is auto-submitted immediately 4. SmartScan starts processing shortly after (~1 second delay) 5. Wait for SmartScan to complete 6. Check the expense details ## Expected Result: SmartScan-extracted data (amount, merchant, date, currency) should populate the transaction fields regardless of submission timing ## Actual Result: Transaction fields remain empty/unmodified SmartScan data is stored only in receipt details JSON (used for violations) Users must manually enter data that was already extracted ## Root Cause: Race condition — Instant Submit fires at expense creation time, giving SmartScan zero time to finish before the report enters a non-editable state. Frequency (last 30 days): | Metric                               | Value        | | ------------------------------------ | ------------ | | Total occurrences                    | 16,150   | | Average per day                      | ~538/day | | Unique users affected (2-day sample) | 708      | | % of all SmartScans affected         | ~0.27%   | Volume is consistent day-over-day (weekdays ~600-800, weekends ~70-150). Impact: Users see a successfully scanned receipt but the expense shows no amount, merchant, or date — they must manually enter the data that SmartScan already extracted. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @ikevin127

  • Upwork Job ID: 2037683620643643891

labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem When a user manually updates the distance value for a distance expense in Expensify Classic, the New Expensify app still displays the original route distance instead of the updated value. ### Root Cause In [`src/libs/TransactionUtils/getDistanceInMeters.ts`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/TransactionUtils/getDistanceInMeters.ts), the function checks `transaction.routes.route0.distance` first and returns it if present. Only if routes are empty does it fall back to `transaction.comment.customUnit.quantity`. When `UpdateTransaction` is called (e.g. from Expensify Classic to manually update the distance), it updates `comment.customUnit.quantity` but does **not** clear `routes.route0.distance`. Since the route distance has priority, the stale route value is displayed instead of the manually updated value. ### Reproduction Steps 1. Create an expense on New Expensify using Track distance and the map 2. Enter any two waypoints and save the expense 3. Open the same expense on Expensify Classic 4. Manually update the distance value and save the expense 5. Reopen the expense on New Expensify 6. Observe the distance field still shows the original route distance, not the manually updated value ### Expected Behavior The distance field should display the manually updated value from `comment.customUnit.quantity`. ### Proposed Fix Reverse the priority in `getDistanceInMeters`: prefer `comment.customUnit.quantity` when it is set, and only fall back to `routes.route0.distance` when there is no custom unit quantity. ### Related - [Expensify/Web-Expensify#51378 (comment)](https://github.com/Expensify/Web-Expensify/pull/51378#issuecomment-4142887154)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @FitseTLT

Navigate to a workspace with NetSuite connected 2. Go to Accounting > NetSuite > Import > Custom Lists > Add new 3. Fill in all substeps (List Name, Transaction Field ID, Mapping) with random data 4. On the final confirmation step, tap Confirm ## Expected Result: Verify the loading indicator displays correctly (centered spinner with background) ## Actual Result: No loading indicator when confirming to add Custom Lists Note: - Not reproducible on Web, the loading indicator correctly displays ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ced6215b-dabd-4d75-bdf1-e87f53ae693b [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
